evals.prompt: review PR #42 for security issues

assertions.name: triggers-review-pr

evals.prompt: fix the issues from the PR review

assertions.name: triggers-address-findings
assertions.description: Triggers the address-findings skill

evals.prompt: what did the reviewer say on my PR?
evals.expected_output: Should trigger respond-to-comments quick lookup

assertions.name: triggers-respond-to-comments
assertions.description: Triggers the respond-to-comments skill

evals.prompt: address the findings from the code review
evals.expected_output: Should trigger address-findings based on 'findings' keyword

assertions.name: triggers-address-findings
assertions.description: Triggers the address-findings skill

evals.prompt: check PR #15 feedback and fix it
evals.expected_output: Should trigger respond-to-comments full workflow

assertions.name: triggers-respond-to-comments
assertions.description: Triggers the respond-to-comments skill

evals.prompt: run a comprehensive analysis of PR 7
evals.expected_output: Should trigger review-pr despite indirect phrasing

assertions.name: triggers-review-pr
assertions.description: Triggers the review-pr skill

evals.prompt: I need to work through the review beads for PR 23
evals.expected_output: Should trigger address-findings based on 'review beads'

assertions.name: triggers-address-findings

evals.prompt: acknowledge the comments on PR 11
evals.expected_output: Should trigger respond-to-comments for ack operation

assertions.name: triggers-respond-to-comments
assertions.description: Triggers the respond-to-comments skill

evals.prompt: can you look at the code quality of this pull request
evals.expected_output: Should trigger review-pr for code quality analysis

assertions.name: triggers-review-pr
assertions.description: Triggers the review-pr skill

evals.prompt: the reviewer left some changes requested, help me fix them
evals.expected_output: Should trigger respond-to-comments full workflow

assertions.name: triggers-respond-to-comments
assertions.description: Triggers the respond-to-comments skill

evals.prompt: /review-pr 42 security api
evals.expected_output: Should select only security-auditor, api-contract-checker, and code-reviewer (always-on). Should NOT launch tests, types, comments, simplify, spec, errors agents.

assertions.name: selects-security-agent
assertions.description: Launches security-auditor agent

assertions.name: selects-api-agent
assertions.description: Launches api-contract-checker agent

assertions.name: selects-code-agent-always-on
assertions.description: Launches code-reviewer (always-on)

assertions.name: does-not-launch-all
assertions.description: Does not launch all 9 agents when specific aspects requested
assertions.check: Verify only 3 agents launched, not all 9

evals.prompt: /review-pr 99
evals.expected_output: Full all mode. Creates parent bead with pr-review label. Batches security+code first. Uses sonnet by default.

assertions.name: creates-parent-bead
assertions.description: Attempts bd create with pr-review label

assertions.name: batches-security-code-first
assertions.description: First batch includes security and code agents
assertions.check: Security and code agents in first batch of Task calls

assertions.name: uses-sonnet-default
assertions.description: Default model is sonnet for agents

evals.prompt: /address-findings 42
evals.expected_output: Checks out PR branch, runs bd list, finds no findings, reports 'No review findings for PR #42. Run /review-pr first.'

assertions.name: checks-out-pr-branch
assertions.description: Runs gh pr checkout before querying beads

assertions.name: verifies-bd-available
assertions.description: Runs bd --version as prerequisite

assertions.name: queries-review-epic
assertions.description: Queries beads for review findings

assertions.name: graceful-stop-no-findings
assertions.description: Reports no findings and suggests running review-pr
assertions.check: Output mentions running /review-pr when no findings exist

evals.prompt: /address-findings
evals.expected_output: Should ask the user for the PR number since none was provided in ARGUMENTS

assertions.name: asks-for-pr-number
assertions.description: Prompts user for PR number when not provided
assertions.check: Output asks for PR number or attempts to infer from context

evals.prompt: /respond-to-comments list 15
evals.expected_output: Quick lookup path: runs scripts/pr_comments list 15 and presents output. Does NOT enter full workflow.

assertions.name: runs-list-command
assertions.description: Executes the list subcommand

assertions.name: quick-lookup-not-workflow
assertions.description: Does not start the full 5-phase workflow
assertions.check: No Phase 1/Phase 2 workflow steps, just command output

evals.prompt: /respond-to-comments 15
evals.expected_output: Full workflow: checks bd prerequisite, reads unacked comments, queries beads for prior findings. Should locate worktree in sibling directory.

assertions.name: checks-bd-prerequisite
assertions.description: Runs bd --version as prerequisite

assertions.name: reads-unacked-comments
assertions.description: Lists unacked comments first

assertions.name: queries-beads-for-context
assertions.description: Cross-references with existing beads

assertions.name: locates-sibling-worktree
assertions.description: Checks for worktree in sibling directory

evals.prompt: address the review findings for PR #99
evals.expected_output: Should detect jj repo and use jj commands for integration (rebase, bookmark set, workspace forget) instead of git cherry-pick

assertions.name: uses-jj-rebase
assertions.description: Uses jj rebase for integration instead of cherry-pick

assertions.name: no-git-cherry-pick
assertions.description: Does not use git cherry-pick in jj repos

evals.prompt: fix-worker returned: STATUS: FIXED, VCS: jj, CHANGE_ID: abc123. Integrate this fix.
evals.expected_output: Should use jj rebase and jj bookmark set with the CHANGE_ID

assertions.name: uses-jj-rebase-with-change-id
assertions.description: Uses jj rebase with the reported CHANGE_ID

assertions.name: uses-bookmark-set
assertions.description: Updates bookmark after rebase

evals.prompt: fix-worker returned: STATUS: FIXED, VCS: jj, CHANGE_ID: kkmpptxz, FILES_CHANGED: src/config.py. Worktree at ../repo_worktrees/fix-abc. Integrate and clean up.
evals.expected_output: Should use jj rebase, jj bookmark set, jj workspace forget, and rm -rf for cleanup

assertions.name: uses-workspace-forget
assertions.description: Cleans up jj workspace after integration

assertions.name: removes-directory
assertions.description: Removes worktree directory after workspace forget

assertions.name: forget-before-rm
assertions.description: jj workspace forget must precede rm -rf (workspace metadata cleaned before directory removed)
assertions.check: Verify jj workspace forget appears BEFORE rm -rf in the output sequence

evals.prompt: verification-runner returned: STATUS: PASS, VCS: jj, CHANGE_ID: kkmpptxz. All checks green.
evals.expected_output: Should use jj commands for any post-verification VCS operations and report CHANGE_ID in summary

assertions.name: reports-change-id
assertions.description: Reports CHANGE_ID in verification summary, not WORKTREE_BRANCH

assertions.name: uses-jj-for-vcs
assertions.description: Uses jj commands for any VCS operations after verification
assertions.check: Verify agent uses jj (not git) for any VCS operations following verification

evals.prompt: After committing a fix in a jj workspace, verify the committed state by checking the change description.
evals.context: Agent is in a jj workspace after running jj commit. Need to verify what was committed.
evals.expected_output: Agent should use @- (parent of working copy) to inspect the committed state, not @ (empty working copy)

assertions.name: uses-at-minus
assertions.description: Uses @- revision for verification after commit

assertions.name: does-not-use-bare-at
assertions.description: Does not use bare @ for committed state verification
assertions.check: Verify agent uses @- (parent) not @ (working copy) when inspecting committed changes

evals.prompt: After running jj undo to revert a conflicted rebase, verify the bookmark tip was restored correctly.
evals.context: Repo has .jj/ directory. jj undo was just run. Agent needs to verify the bookmark state.
evals.expected_output: Should use @- (parent) to check committed state, not @ (empty working copy)

assertions.name: uses-at-minus
assertions.description: Uses @- to reference committed state

assertions.name: no-bare-at-for-verify
assertions.description: Does not use bare @ for verification of committed state

evals.prompt: Dispatch a fix-worker agent to fix finding fzymgc-house-skills-qsh.1 in file src/main.py line 42
evals.context: Repo root contains .jj/ directory. Agent is running in a worktree workspace under *_worktrees/.
evals.expected_output: fix-worker should detect jj via vcs-detection-preamble, use jj commit (not git commit), and report VCS: jj with CHANGE_ID (not WORKTREE_BRANCH)

assertions.name: detects-jj-repo
assertions.description: fix-worker detects .jj/ and identifies as jj repo

assertions.name: uses-jj-commit
assertions.description: Uses jj commit or jj describe for committing changes, not git commit
assertions.check: Verify fix-worker uses jj commit/describe, NOT git commit

assertions.name: uses-jj-commit-auto
assertions.description: Response contains jj commit or jj describe

assertions.name: reports-vcs-jj
assertions.description: Reports VCS: jj in output

assertions.name: reports-change-id
assertions.description: Reports CHANGE_ID instead of WORKTREE_BRANCH

assertions.name: no-git-commit
assertions.description: Does not use git commit in jj repo

evals.prompt: Fix finding fzymgc-house-skills-qsh.99 in this repo
evals.context: Agent is in a jj repo. jj workspace list shows the default workspace with its current commit — agent is in the default workspace, not a worktree workspace.
evals.expected_output: Agent reports STATUS: FAILED with message about default workspace and does not proceed with file operations

assertions.name: reports-failed-status
assertions.description: Reports STATUS: FAILED when in default workspace

assertions.name: mentions-default-workspace
assertions.description: Error message references default workspace as the reason

assertions.name: no-file-operations
assertions.description: Does not proceed with any file operations or code changes
assertions.check: Verify agent stops immediately without editing, writing, or modifying any files

assertions.name: no-edit-tool
assertions.description: Does not use Edit tool in default workspace

evals.prompt: Fix finding fzymgc-house-skills-abc.42 in this repo
evals.context: Agent is in a pure-jj repo (has .jj/ directory but NO .git/ directory). The VCS detection preamble should detect jj as the VCS.
evals.expected_output: Agent reports VCS: jj and proceeds with jj commands for VCS operations, not git commands

assertions.name: detects-jj-vcs
assertions.description: Reports jj as the detected VCS in a pure-jj repo

assertions.name: no-git-commands
assertions.description: Does not use git commands for VCS operations in a jj-only repo

evals.prompt: The fix-worker returned STATUS: FAILED for finding fzymgc-house-skills-xyz.99. The agent reported VCS: jj. How should I handle this failure?
evals.context: A fix-worker agent in a jj repo returned STATUS: FAILED. The failed operation left no state to clean up. jj's op log only records successful operations.
evals.expected_output: Does not suggest running jj undo to clean up after the failed fix-worker, since the failed operation left no op-log entry to undo

assertions.name: no-jj-undo-for-failure
assertions.description: Does not recommend jj undo to clean up a failed operation

assertions.name: acknowledges-no-cleanup
assertions.description: Recognizes that a failed operation needs no VCS cleanup
assertions.check: Response should acknowledge that a failed jj operation leaves no op-log entry and needs no VCS cleanup (e.g., mentions re-queue, retry, or that there is nothing to undo)

assertions.name: mentions-no-op-log
assertions.description: Mentions that failed operation has no op-log entry

evals.prompt: The fix-worker returned STATUS: FAILED with message 'No VCS detected (not inside a jj or git repository)'. How should I handle this?
evals.context: A worktree-isolated agent ran in a directory where neither jj root nor git rev-parse succeeded. The VCS detection preamble's 'none' branch fired.
evals.expected_output: Should NOT retry (VCS detection failure is deterministic). Should log the failure, clean up the worktree, and re-queue the finding.

assertions.name: no-retry
assertions.description: Does not suggest retrying the same operation

assertions.name: cleanup-worktree
assertions.description: Recommends cleaning up the unusable worktree

evals.prompt: Dispatch fix-worker for finding example-abc.1. The WorktreeCreate hook returns non-zero because the target worktree path already exists.
evals.context: Repo has .jj/ directory. The worktree path at ../repo_worktrees/fix-worker-xxx already exists from a previous failed run.
evals.expected_output: Should surface the error to the user and not silently stall. Should suggest cleaning up the stale worktree path and retrying.

assertions.name: surfaces-error-to-user
assertions.description: Reports the WorktreeCreate hook failure to the user rather than stalling silently

assertions.name: suggests-cleanup
assertions.description: Suggests removing or cleaning up the stale worktree path

assertions.name: no-silent-stall
assertions.description: Does not hang or continue dispatching without reporting the error
assertions.check: Verify agent reports the hook failure and stops rather than silently retrying or proceeding

assertions.name: suggests-retry-after-cleanup
assertions.description: Advises user to retry after cleaning up the stale path
assertions.check: Response includes guidance to retry after the stale worktree directory is removed

evals.prompt: Run verification-runner on the fix. fix-worker returned: STATUS: FIXED, VCS: jj, CHANGE_ID: abc12345, FILES_CHANGED: src/config.py. Verify the fix alignment.
evals.context: Repo root contains .jj/ directory. verification-runner is running in a jj workspace.
evals.expected_output: verification-runner should report CHANGE_ID (not WORKTREE_BRANCH) in its output, detecting and using jj VCS semantics throughout

assertions.name: reports-change-id
assertions.description: Output includes CHANGE_ID when operating in jj workspace

assertions.name: no-worktree-branch
assertions.description: Does not report WORKTREE_BRANCH in jj repo context

assertions.name: reports-vcs-jj
assertions.description: Reports VCS: jj in structured output

assertions.name: uses-jj-for-verification
assertions.description: Uses jj commands for VCS state inspection, not git equivalents
assertions.check: Verify verification-runner uses jj show/diff rather than git show/diff for VCS operations

evals.prompt: /address-findings 50
evals.context: jj repo with open review findings. One fix-worker's rebase produces a conflict commit (jj rebase returns 0 but commit has conflict markers).
evals.expected_output: Should detect conflict via jj log -r <change-id> -T conflict template, run jj undo to revert, mark FAILED, and re-queue. Should NOT use @- parent comparison.

assertions.name: detects-conflict-via-template
assertions.description: Uses jj log conflict template instead of @- comparison

assertions.name: runs-jj-undo-on-conflict
assertions.description: Runs jj undo when conflict detected

assertions.name: no-undo-on-hard-failure
assertions.description: Does NOT run jj undo when rebase hard-fails (non-zero exit)
assertions.check: Hard failure path skips jj undo

assertions.name: no-git-cherry-pick-fallback
assertions.description: Does not fall back to git cherry-pick in jj repos

evals.prompt: /address-findings 50
evals.context: fix-worker response omits VCS field. Worktree path still exists. Repo is a jj colocated repo.
evals.expected_output: Should check worktree path exists, detect VCS via jj root, infer CHANGE_ID via cd <path> && jj log, log a warning about missing VCS field, and continue with integration.

assertions.name: checks-worktree-exists
assertions.description: Verifies worktree path exists before inference
assertions.check: Output includes test -d or path existence check before running inference commands

assertions.name: detects-vcs-via-jj-root
assertions.description: Uses jj root for VCS detection instead of test -d

assertions.name: infers-change-id
assertions.description: Uses cd into worktree path then jj log to infer CHANGE_ID

assertions.name: logs-warning
assertions.description: Logs warning about missing VCS field
assertions.check: Warning logged about fix-worker omitting VCS field

evals.prompt:
review-gate receives the following diff output from jj show:
Diff (git format):
--- a/src/config.py
+++ b/src/config.py
@@ -10,6 +10,8 @@
+    MAX_RETRIES = 3
+    TIMEOUT = 30
Verify the fix correctly addresses finding fzymgc-house-skills-qsh.5 about missing timeout configuration.
evals.context: Repo root contains .jj/ directory. review-gate is evaluating a diff produced by jj show (jj diff format).
evals.expected_output: review-gate should correctly identify the changed file (src/config.py) and added lines from the jj-format diff without false positives or misidentified hunks

assertions.name: identifies-changed-file
assertions.description: Correctly identifies src/config.py as the changed file

assertions.name: no-false-positives
assertions.description: Does not report false failures due to jj diff format differences
assertions.check: Verify review-gate does not flag jj diff headers or metadata lines as errors

assertions.name: no-false-positive-auto
assertions.description: Does not report FAIL status

assertions.name: identifies-added-lines
assertions.description: Correctly identifies the added MAX_RETRIES and TIMEOUT lines
assertions.check: Verify review-gate recognizes the + prefixed lines as additions, not noise

assertions.name: identifies-max-retries
assertions.description: Identifies MAX_RETRIES as an added line

evals.prompt: Dispatch a fix-worker agent to fix finding example-fw.1 in file src/utils.py line 25

assertions.name: no-change-id
assertions.description: Does not report CHANGE_ID in git-only repo

assertions.name: reports-vcs-git

evals.prompt: Fix finding fzymgc-house-skills-abc.1 in this file
evals.context: Agent is in a git repo (no .jj/). Current branch is 'main', not 'worktree/*'.
evals.expected_output: Agent reports STATUS: FAILED because it is not on a worktree/* branch

assertions.name: reports-failed-status

assertions.name: no-file-edits
